sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal, as rightly observed by Manger on Hos 2:21 pr. i.q. עָרַשׂ, عرش to erect, to build, whence עֶרֶשׂ a bed, couch with a canopy. From the idea of a bed-fellow عَرْسُ a husband or wife, عَرُوسُ one espoused; hence-

Piel, אֵרֵשׂ to espouse a woman; pr. to make a spouse. Constr. with אֵרֵשׂ אִשָּׁה Deu 20:7 28:30 אֵרֵשׂ לֹו אִשָּׁה Hos 2:21, 22 2Sa 3:14. There is added בּ of price [paid for the wife] 2 Sa. loc. cit.

Pual, אֹרַשׂ f. in Pause אֹרָשָׂה to be betrothed, Exo 22:15 Deu 22:28. Part. מְאֹרָשָׂה, Deu, 22:23 , 25 Deu 22:25, 27 Deu 22:27. (Ch. אֲרַס Pi. and Pa. id).
